title: pyrus vitis-idaea
intro: Pyrus vitis-idaea, also known as the mountain cranberry, is a low-growing evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pyrus vitis-idaea include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's berries, leaves, and roots can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to leverage its medicinal properties.
